book.smithery.smithery_book.forge.alloyi:
When the right molten metals share a hot enough forge, they combine on their own. **Netherite** forms from molten gold and ancient debris at 2,200°C.

Toggle auto-alloying with the **A** button on the controller screen if you want to keep your metals separate.
Alloy recipes are data-driven — modpacks add their own. Check **JEI** for the full list, and watch item tooltips: anything meltable shows what it melts into and which alloys want it.
book.smithery.smithery_book.forge.buildi:
The smallest legal forge: a hollow shell of **Furnace Bricks** around one interior air block, with a **Controller**, a **Fuel Port** and a **Drain** in the walls.

Use the anchor button to project this ghost into your world and build along it.

The shell can be almost any shape:

- Corners are **optional**
- The top can be **open or closed** — closed heats 20% faster
- Partial structures still run at reduced effectiveness

Every interior air block adds one working slot and **1,000 mB** of molten storage. Right-click the controller to see everything at once.
book.smithery.smithery_book.forge.castin: **Casting Sand** — the consumable half of sand casting. Eight sand around a clay ball makes eight.

Place Casting Sand on a **Casting Table**, then press any existing part into the sand to impress its shape. Templates are never consumed.

No part yet? A vanilla **ingot** impresses the ingot shape, **flint** a sharpening stone, a **brick** a polishing stone.

Pour molten metal onto the impressed sand — bucket or pipe, from above. It fills, then cools from glowing to solid.

Sweep the sand away with a **brush** and take your part, or let a hopper collect it underneath.
book.smithery.smithery_book.forge.draini:
Pick the output fluid by clicking its layer in the controller's tank, then:

- **Bucket** it straight from the Drain, or
- **Power the Drain with redstone** to pump into connected Fluid Pipes — 5 mB per tick along the network, straight to your casting tables.
book.smithery.smithery_book.forge.fuel.p:
Fuel Ports hold **liquid fuel** — bucket it in or pipe it. Ports stacked vertically merge into one tank per fluid.

- **Lava** burns toward 1,650°C
- **Molten Blaze** reaches 3,500°C for the hottest melts

The forge heats toward its fuel's target and cools when starved. Every material has a melting point — **iron needs 1,538°C**, so a fresh lava forge warms up for a moment before iron starts to drip.

Hotter forges melt *faster*, not just hotter. Blaze fuel pays for itself.
book.smithery.smithery_book.forge.meltin:
Drop items into the forge interior, or feed them through an **Item Port**. Once the forge passes their melting point they liquefy:

- Nugget: **16 mB**
- Ingot: **144 mB**
- Raw ore: **288 mB** — *double yield!*
- Block: **1,296 mB**

Living creatures inside a hot forge are **scalded**, dripping fluid with every tick of damage. Most mobs render down to blood.

Some render down to... other things. Experiment respectfully.
book.smithery.smithery_book.forge.name: The Forge
book.smithery.smithery_book.getting_star:
Before you can melt anything, the **Part Press** cuts parts from wood and flint. Press a pick head, two handles, and a binder from logs, then drop all four parts into a crafting grid together.

The parts sort themselves — **no shape to memorize**.

Hover any smithery tool:

- Hold **Shift** for stats, parts, modifiers and synergies
- Hold **Shift + Ctrl** for every parameter

Durability, mining speed and damage all come from the parts you chose. There is no such thing as a plain iron pickaxe — only *your* pickaxe.

Smithery replaces fixed recipes with **parts**. Every tool and armor piece is assembled from interchangeable parts, and every part carries the stats and traits of its material.

An **iron** pick head mines like iron. Put it on **slime** handles and the tool bounces back from its own mistakes. Materials matter more than shapes.

1. Build a **Forge** and melt raw materials into molten metal
2. **Cast** parts on the Casting Table — or **press** non-metals on the Part Press
3. **Assemble** the parts in any crafting grid
4. **Upgrade** at an anvil: modifiers, repairs, embossments

Each step has its own chapter in this guide.

book.smithery.smithery_book.materials.tr:
Materials grant traits to specific tools and armor **for free** — no modifier slots spent. Iron pickaxes magnetize drops; slime boots bounce; blaze armor shrugs off fire.

Every material's entry in this chapter lists its grants.

Mixing specific materials in one tool unlocks bonus modifiers at no cost:

- **Galvanic** — iron + copper
- **Gilded** — iron + gold
- **Verdant Veil** — copper + gold

Experiment with mixed-material builds.
book.smithery.smithery_book.materials.wo:

- Durability: **35** per ingot
- Does not melt — cut it on the **Part Press**
book.smithery.smithery_book.modifiers.ar:
Reduces all damage taken per level, per piece.

**Apply with:** Amethyst Shard at an anvil.

Reduces fire damage taken per level, per piece.

**Apply with:** Magma Cream at an anvil.

Swing faster while worn.

**Apply with:** Feather at an anvil.

Reach farther while worn.

**Apply with:** End Rod at an anvil.

Reduces explosion damage taken per level, per piece.

**Apply with:** Tnt at an anvil.

Reduces projectile damage taken per level, per piece.

**Apply with:** Turtle Scute at an anvil.

Increases movement speed per level while worn.

**Apply with:** Sugar at an anvil.

Step up full blocks without jumping.

**Apply with:** Piston at an anvil.

Air lasts longer underwater.

**Apply with:** Prismarine Crystals at an anvil.

Slowly repairs itself using your experience.

**Apply with:** Moss Block at an anvil.

Freezes water beneath your steps.

**Apply with:** Blue Ice at an anvil.

Hit harder while worn.

**Apply with:** Blaze Rod at an anvil.
book.smithery.smithery_book.modifiers.em: Place **any smithery part** in an anvil with a finished tool or armor piece to emboss it: the part's material grants its *traits* to the gear without changing any stats. The part is consumed.

Re-embossing with a new part **replaces** the old donor, so grafted traits grow with your progression — emboss iron today, netherite next month.

Embossed traits never override anything the gear already has; they only fill gaps.
book.smithery.smithery_book.modifiers.ho:
A tool's modifier capacity comes from its **binder** material; armor's from its **plates**:

- Iron: 2 slots
- Gold: 3 slots
- Netherite: 4 slots
- Bedrock: 5 slots

Each modifier level spends one slot. Traits granted by materials are **free** and never spend slots.

Combine gear with a modifier item at any **anvil**. Costs scale per level, and part-way contributions are remembered — you can pay a level's cost across several visits.

Vanilla enchanted books are refused; smithery gear speaks modifiers.
book.smithery.smithery_book.modifiers.re:
Cast a **Sharpening Stone** (1 ingot) for tools or a **Polishing Stone** (2 ingots) for armor, then combine with your gear at an anvil.

Each stone restores a quarter of max durability; the anvil only consumes what the damage needs.

A stone only works gear at or below its material's harvest level — wood cannot hold an edge on netherite.

Broken armor **never shatters**: it stays equipped, granting nothing, until you repair it back to life.
book.smithery.smithery_book.modifiers.to:
Adds bonus attack damage on hit.

**Apply with:** Quartz at an anvil.

Each level adds +1.5 mining and attack speed.

**Apply with:** Redstone at an anvil.

Chance to teleport your target a short distance on hit.

**Apply with:** Ender Pearl at an anvil.

Stays with you through death.

**Apply with:** Totem Of Undying at an anvil.

Each level boosts ore drops and mob drops, scaling like Fortune and Looting.

**Apply with:** Lapis Lazuli at an anvil.

Harvests blocks intact.

**Apply with:** Emerald at an anvil.

Hits send targets flying.

**Apply with:** Slime Ball at an anvil.

Sets targets on fire.

**Apply with:** Blaze Powder at an anvil.

Increases durability per level.

**Apply with:** Obsidian at an anvil.

Widens the mining hammer's break area.

**Apply with:** Sticky Piston at an anvil.

Adds significant bonus attack damage.

**Apply with:** Nether Star at an anvil.

Spear attack hurls you forward; consumes hunger. Requires food ≥ 7, on solid ground, out of water..

**Apply with:** Phantom Membrane at an anvil.
book.smithery.smithery_book.name: Smithery Field Guide
book.smithery.smithery_book.part_press.p:
Wood, flint, slime, resin and coral never melt — they get **cut**.

Right-click the press with your input (logs, flint, slime balls, resin clumps, coral blocks), and right-click the frame to cycle which part shape it cuts.

A **redstone pulse** drives the press down and cuts the input into the selected part. Hoppers feed inputs and collect outputs while the press is open — a redstone clock turns it into a part factory.

Early game this is your only source of handles and binders: wood first, metal later.
book.smithery.smithery_book.tooltip: Melting, casting, pressing, assembling — all of it.
